34: 	readonly executionTargetLabel?: string;
35: 	readonly externalUrl?: string;
36: 	readonly cloudProviderId?: string;
37: 	readonly openSessionCommand:
38: 		| { readonly kind: "agent-chat"; readonly sessionId: string }
39: 		| {
40: 				readonly kind: "cloud-agent";
41: 				readonly localId: string;
42: 				readonly externalUrl?: string;
43: 		  };
44: }
45: 
46: export interface OrchestrationSnapshot {
47: 	readonly sessions: readonly OrchestrationSessionProjection[];
48: 	readonly activeProvider:
49: 		| {
50: 				readonly id: string;
51: 				readonly displayName: string;
52: 		  }
53: 		| undefined;
54: 	readonly generatedAt: number;
55: 	readonly degradedReasons: readonly string[];
56: }
57: 
58: export interface OrchestrationReadModelOptions {
59: 	readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
60: 	readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
61: 	readonly cloudSessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage;
62: 	readonly cloudProviderRegistry?: ProviderRegistry;
63: 	readonly agentChatStoreChangeEvent: Event<unknown>;
64: 	readonly onCloudSessionsChanged?: (listener: () => void) => {
65: 		dispose(): void;
66: 	};
67: 	readonly onCloudProviderChanged?: (listener: () => void) => {
68: 		dispose(): void;
69: 	};
70: 	readonly now?: () => number;
71: }
72: 
73: export class OrchestrationReadModel {
74: 	private readonly store: AgentChatSessionStore;
75: 	private readonly registry: AgentChatRegistry;
76: 	private readonly cloudSessionStorage?: AgentSessionStorage;
77: 	private readonly cloudProviderRegistry?: ProviderRegistry;
78: 	private readonly now: () => number;
79: 	private readonly subscriptions: Array<{ dispose(): void }> = [];
80: 	private readonly listeners = new Set<() => void>();
81: 
82: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationReadModelOptions) {
83: 		this.store = options.store;
84: 		this.registry = options.registry;
85: 		this.cloudSessionStorage = options.cloudSessionStorage;
86: 		this.cloudProviderRegistry = options.cloudProviderRegistry;
87: 		this.now = options.now ?? Date.now;
88: 
89: 		this.subscriptions.push(
90: 			options.agentChatStoreChangeEvent(() => {
91: 				this.emitChanged();
92: 			})
93: 		);
94: 		this.subscriptions.push(
95: 			this.registry.onDidChange(() => {
96: 				this.emitChanged();
97: 			})
98: 		);
99: 		if (options.onCloudSessionsChanged) {
100: 			this.subscriptions.push(
101: 				options.onCloudSessionsChanged(() => {
102: 					this.emitChanged();
103: 				})
104: 			);
105: 		}
106: 		if (options.onCloudProviderChanged) {
107: 			this.subscriptions.push(
108: 				options.onCloudProviderChanged(() => {
109: 					this.emitChanged();
110: 				})
111: 			);
112: 		}
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	onDidChange(listener: () => void): { dispose(): void } {
116: 		this.listeners.add(listener);
117: 		return {
118: 			dispose: () => {
119: 				this.listeners.delete(listener);
120: 			},
121: 		};
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	async snapshot(): Promise<OrchestrationSnapshot> {
125: 		const sessions: OrchestrationSessionProjection[] = [];
126: 		const degradedReasons: string[] = [];
127: 
128: 		const [agentChatSessions, cloudSessions] = await Promise.all([
129: 			this.collectAgentChatSessions(),
130: 			this.collectCloudSessions(degradedReasons),
131: 		]);
132: 
133: 		sessions.push(...agentChatSessions, ...cloudSessions);
134: 		sessions.sort(compareSessions);
135: 
136: 		const activeProvider = this.cloudProviderRegistry?.getActive();
137: 
138: 		return {
139: 			sessions,
140: 			activeProvider: activeProvider
141: 				? {
142: 						id: activeProvider.metadata.id,
143: 						displayName: activeProvider.metadata.displayName,
144: 					}
145: 				: undefined,
146: 			generatedAt: this.now(),
147: 			degradedReasons,
148: 		};
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	dispose(): void {
152: 		for (const subscription of this.subscriptions) {
153: 			try {
154: 				subscription.dispose();
155: 			} catch {
156: 				// best-effort
157: 			}
158: 		}
159: 		this.subscriptions.length = 0;
160: 		this.listeners.clear();
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	private emitChanged(): void {
164: 		for (const listener of [...this.listeners]) {
165: 			try {
166: 				listener();
167: 			} catch {
168: 				// best-effort
169: 			}
170: 		}
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	private async collectAgentChatSessions(): Promise<
174: 		OrchestrationSessionProjection[]
175: 	> {
176: 		const [active, recent] = await Promise.all([
177: 			this.store.listActive(),
178: 			this.store.listRecent(),
179: 		]);
180: 		return [...active, ...recent].map((session) =>
181: 			toAgentChatProjection(session)
182: 		);
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	private async collectCloudSessions(
186: 		degradedReasons: string[]
187: 	): Promise<OrchestrationSessionProjection[]> {
188: 		if (!this.cloudSessionStorage) {
189: 			degradedReasons.push("Cloud agent session storage is unavailable.");
190: 			return [];
191: 		}
192: 		const sessions = await this.cloudSessionStorage.getAll();
193: 		return sessions.map((session) => toCloudProjection(session));
194: 	}
195: }
196: 
197: function toAgentChatProjection(
198: 	session: AgentChatSession
199: ): OrchestrationSessionProjection {
200: 	return {
201: 		id: `agent-chat:${session.id}`,
202: 		source: "agent-chat",
203: 		sourceSessionId: session.id,
204: 		title: session.selectedModeId
205: 			? `${session.agentDisplayName} (${session.selectedModeId})`
206: 			: session.agentDisplayName,
207: 		agentName: session.agentDisplayName,
208: 		state: session.lifecycleState,
209: 		bucket: bucketForAgentChat(session.lifecycleState),
210: 		createdAt: session.createdAt,
211: 		updatedAt: session.updatedAt,
212: 		endedAt: session.endedAt,
213: 		lastVisibleActivityAt: session.updatedAt,
214: 		isBlocked: session.lifecycleState === "waiting-for-input",
215: 		worktreeStatus: session.worktree?.status,
216: 		executionTargetLabel: executionTargetLabel(session.executionTarget.kind),
217: 		externalUrl: session.cloud?.externalUrl,
218: 		cloudProviderId: session.cloud?.providerId,
219: 		openSessionCommand: { kind: "agent-chat", sessionId: session.id },
220: 	};
221: }
222: 
223: function toCloudProjection(
224: 	session: AgentSession
225: ): OrchestrationSessionProjection {
226: 	const firstTask = session.tasks[0];
227: 	const title = firstTask
228: 		? `${firstTask.specTaskId}: ${firstTask.title}`
229: 		: session.specPath;
230: 	return {
231: 		id: `cloud-agent:${session.localId}`,
232: 		source: "cloud-agent",
233: 		sourceSessionId: session.localId,
234: 		title,
235: 		agentName: session.providerId,
236: 		state: session.status,
237: 		bucket: bucketForCloud(session.status),
238: 		createdAt: session.createdAt,
239: 		updatedAt: session.updatedAt,
240: 		endedAt: session.completedAt,
241: 		lastVisibleActivityAt: session.updatedAt,
242: 		isBlocked: session.status === SessionStatus.BLOCKED,
243: 		worktreeStatus: session.isReadOnly ? "read-only" : undefined,
244: 		executionTargetLabel: "Cloud",
245: 		externalUrl: session.externalUrl,
246: 		cloudProviderId: session.providerId,
247: 		openSessionCommand: {
248: 			kind: "cloud-agent",
249: 			localId: session.localId,
250: 			externalUrl: session.externalUrl,
251: 		},
252: 	};
253: }
254: 
255: function bucketForAgentChat(
256: 	state: SessionLifecycleState
257: ): OrchestrationSessionBucket {
258: 	if (state === "waiting-for-input") {
259: 		return "waiting";
260: 	}
261: 	if (state === "completed") {
262: 		return "completed";
263: 	}
264: 	if (
265: 		state === "failed" ||
266: 		state === "cancelled" ||
267: 		state === "ended-by-shutdown"
268: 	) {
269: 		return "failed";
270: 	}
271: 	return "active";
272: }
273: 
274: function bucketForCloud(
275: 	status: AgentSession["status"]
276: ): OrchestrationSessionBucket {
277: 	if (status === SessionStatus.BLOCKED) {
278: 		return "waiting";
279: 	}
280: 	if (status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED) {
281: 		return "completed";
282: 	}
283: 	if (status === SessionStatus.FAILED || status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED) {
284: 		return "failed";
285: 	}
286: 	return "active";
287: }
288: 
289: function executionTargetLabel(kind: "local" | "worktree" | "cloud"): string {
290: 	if (kind === "worktree") {
291: 		return "Worktree";
292: 	}
293: 	if (kind === "cloud") {
294: 		return "Cloud";
295: 	}
296: 	return "Local";
297: }
298: 
299: function compareSessions(
300: 	a: OrchestrationSessionProjection,
301: 	b: OrchestrationSessionProjection
302: ): number {
303: 	if (a.bucket !== b.bucket) {
304: 		return bucketRank(a.bucket) - bucketRank(b.bucket);
305: 	}
306: 	return b.lastVisibleActivityAt - a.lastVisibleActivityAt;
307: }
308: 
309: function bucketRank(bucket: OrchestrationSessionBucket): number {
310: 	if (bucket === "active") {
311: 		return 0;
312: 	}
313: 	if (bucket === "waiting") {
314: 		return 1;
315: 	}
316: 	if (bucket === "completed") {
317: 		return 2;
318: 	}
319: 	return 3;
320: }

1: import {

3: 	env,
4: 	type CancellationToken,
5: 	type Disposable,
6: 	type ExtensionContext,

11: } from "vscode";

13: 	AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS,
14: 	logTelemetry,
15: } from "../features/agent-chat/telemetry";
16: import type {
17: 	OrchestrationReadModel,
18: 	OrchestrationSessionProjection,
19: } from "../features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model";
20: import { getWebviewContent } from "../utils/get-webview-content";
21: 
22: interface OrchestrationViewProviderOptions {
23: 	readonly context: ExtensionContext;
24: 	readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
25: 	readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };

28: export class OrchestrationViewProvider implements WebviewViewProvider {
29: 	static readonly viewType = "gatomia.views.orchestration";
30: 
31: 	private readonly context: ExtensionContext;
32: 	private readonly readModel: OrchestrationReadModel;
33: 	private readonly outputChannel?: { appendLine(value: string): void };
34: 	private readonly disposables: Disposable[] = [];
35: 	private view: WebviewView | undefined;
36: 
37: 	constructor(options: OrchestrationViewProviderOptions) {
38: 		this.context = options.context;
39: 		this.readModel = options.readModel;
40: 		this.outputChannel = options.outputChannel;
41: 
42: 		this.disposables.push(
43: 			this.readModel.onDidChange(() => {
44: 				this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
45: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
46: 						`[Orchestration] snapshot push failed: ${formatError(error)}`
47: 					);
48: 				});
49: 			})
50: 		);
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	resolveWebviewView(
54: 		webviewView: WebviewView,
55: 		_context: WebviewViewResolveContext,
56: 		_token: CancellationToken
57: 	): void {
58: 		this.view = webviewView;
59: 
60: 		webviewView.webview.options = {
61: 			enableScripts: true,
62: 			localResourceRoots: [this.context.extensionUri],
63: 		};
64: 		webviewView.webview.html = getWebviewContent(
65: 			webviewView.webview,
66: 			this.context.extensionUri,
67: 			"orchestration"
68: 		);
69: 
70: 		this.disposables.push(
71: 			webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage((message) => {
72: 				this.handleWebviewMessage(message).catch((error) => {
73: 					this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
74: 						`[Orchestration] message handling failed: ${formatError(error)}`
75: 					);
76: 				});
77: 			})
78: 		);
79: 
80: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
81: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
82: 				`[Orchestration] initial snapshot failed: ${formatError(error)}`
83: 			);
84: 		});
85: 	}
86: 
87: 	async reveal(): Promise<void> {
88: 		await commands.executeCommand(
89: 			`${OrchestrationViewProvider.viewType}.focus`
90: 		);
91: 		this.view?.show?.(true);
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	refresh(): void {
95: 		this.pushSnapshot().catch((error) => {
96: 			this.outputChannel?.appendLine(
97: 				`[Orchestration] refresh failed: ${formatError(error)}`
98: 			);
99: 		});
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	dispose(): void {
103: 		for (const disposable of this.disposables) {
104: 			try {
105: 				disposable.dispose();
106: 			} catch {
107: 				// best-effort
108: 			}
109: 		}
110: 		this.disposables.length = 0;
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	private async handleWebviewMessage(raw: unknown): Promise<void> {
114: 		const message = raw as { type?: string; payload?: unknown };
115: 		if (!message?.type) {
116: 			return;
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		switch (message.type) {
120: 			case "orchestration/ready":
121: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
122: 				return;
123: 			case "orchestration/refresh":
124: 				await commands
125: 					.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents")
126: 					.catch(() => {
127: 						// best-effort
128: 					});
129: 				await this.pushSnapshot();
130: 				return;
131: 			case "orchestration/open-session": {
132: 				const sessionId = (
133: 					message.payload as { sessionId?: string } | undefined
134: 				)?.sessionId;
135: 				if (!sessionId) {
136: 					return;
137: 				}
138: 				const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
139: 				const session = snapshot.sessions.find(
140: 					(entry) => entry.id === sessionId
141: 				);
142: 				if (session) {
143: 					await this.openSession(session);
144: 				}
145: 				return;
146: 			}
147: 			case "orchestration/open-existing-surface": {
148: 				const source = (message.payload as { source?: string } | undefined)
149: 					?.source;
150: 				if (source === "cloud-agent") {
151: 					await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
152: 					return;
153: 				}
154: 				await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.agentChat.openPanel");
155: 				return;
156: 			}
157: 			case "orchestration/open-external": {
158: 				const url = (message.payload as { url?: string } | undefined)?.url;
159: 				if (url) {
160: 					await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(url));
161: 				}
162: 				return;
163: 			}
164: 			default:
165: 				return;
166: 		}
167: 	}
168: 
169: 	private async pushSnapshot(): Promise<void> {
170: 		if (!this.view) {
171: 			return;
172: 		}
173: 		const snapshot = await this.readModel.snapshot();
174: 		await this.view.webview.postMessage({
175: 			type: "orchestration/snapshot",
176: 			payload: snapshot,
177: 		});
178: 	}
179: 
180: 	private async openSession(
181: 		session: OrchestrationSessionProjection
182: 	): Promise<void> {
183: 		logTelemetry(AGENT_CHAT_TELEMETRY_EVENTS.PANEL_OPENED, {
184: 			sessionId: session.sourceSessionId,
185: 			source: session.source,
186: 			surface: "orchestration",
187: 		});
188: 
189: 		if (session.openSessionCommand.kind === "agent-chat") {
190: 			await commands.executeCommand(
191: 				"gatomia.agentChat.openForSession",
192: 				session.openSessionCommand.sessionId
193: 			);
194: 			return;
195: 		}
196: 
197: 		if (session.externalUrl) {
198: 			await env.openExternal(Uri.parse(session.externalUrl));
199: 			return;
200: 		}
201: 
202: 		await commands.executeCommand("gatomia.refreshCloudAgents");
203: 	}
204: }
205: 
206: function formatError(error: unknown): string {
207: 	return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
208: }

1: /**
2:  * Agent Chat Panel shared types.
3:  *
4:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/data-model.md (source of truth)
5:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/ for the panel protocol, capabilities,
6:  *      session-storage, and worktree-lifecycle contracts.
7:  *
8:  * This module is the single authoritative declaration for every entity persisted
9:  * by `AgentChatSessionStore` or shipped across the webview bridge. The
10:  * webview-side module `ui/src/features/agent-chat/types.ts` MUST mirror the
11:  * subset that crosses the postMessage boundary.
12:  */
13: 
14: // ============================================================================
15: // Session lifecycle
16: // ============================================================================
17: 
18: export type SessionLifecycleState =
19: 	| "initializing"
20: 	| "running"
21: 	| "waiting-for-input"
22: 	| "completed"
23: 	| "failed"
24: 	| "cancelled"
25: 	| "ended-by-shutdown";
26: 
27: /**
28:  * Lifecycle states that are absorbing — once entered, no further transitions
29:  * are permitted. A new run of "the same" task is a new session with a new id.
30:  *
31:  * @see data-model.md §2
32:  */
33: export const TERMINAL_STATES: ReadonlySet<SessionLifecycleState> =
34: 	new Set<SessionLifecycleState>([
35: 		"completed",
36: 		"failed",
37: 		"cancelled",
38: 		"ended-by-shutdown",
39: 	]);
40: 
41: export function isTerminalState(state: SessionLifecycleState): boolean {
42: 	return TERMINAL_STATES.has(state);
43: }
44: 
45: // ============================================================================
46: // Capabilities (hybrid discovery)
47: // ============================================================================
48: 
49: export interface ModeDescriptor {
50: 	/** Machine id (e.g. "code", "ask", "plan"). */
51: 	id: string;
52: 	/** UI label (e.g. "Code"). */
53: 	displayName: string;
54: 	/**
55: 	 * Prefix prepended to each turn's first message to steer the agent.
56: 	 * Research R1: used when the agent does not support a native mode switch.
57: 	 */
58: 	promptPrefix?: string;
59: }
60: 
61: export interface ModelDescriptor {
62: 	id: string;
63: 	displayName: string;
64: 	/** How the model is communicated to the agent at session start. */
65: 	invocation: "initial-prompt" | "cli-flag";
66: 	/** Optional template (e.g. "--model {id}" for cli-flag). */
67: 	invocationTemplate?: string;
68: }
69: 
70: /**
71:  * Reasoning / thinking effort level surfaced by the agent.
72:  *
73:  * Distinct from {@link ModeDescriptor} (which is the high-level
74:  * conversational mode like "Agent / Plan / Ask") and from
75:  * {@link ModelDescriptor} (which selects the underlying LLM). Examples:
76:  *
77:  *   - GPT-5 / Codex: `low`, `medium`, `high`
78:  *   - Claude Extended Thinking: `off`, `on` (or budget tiers)
79:  *   - Qwen Thinking: `disabled`, `enabled`
80:  *
81:  * Optional per provider; agents that do not expose a knob omit the
82:  * `thinkingLevels` array entirely so the chip is hidden.
83:  */
84: export interface ThinkingLevelDescriptor {
85: 	id: string;
86: 	displayName: string;
87: 	description?: string;
88: }
89: 
90: /**
91:  * High-level conversational role / agent type the user can pick before
92:  * a turn (e.g. Cursor's "Agent / Ask / Edit", Windsurf's "Cascade Auto
93:  * vs Cascade Plan"). Persisted alongside the session selection so each
94:  * turn can carry the user's intent.
95:  */
96: export interface AgentRoleDescriptor {
97: 	id: string;
98: 	displayName: string;
99: 	description?: string;
100: }
101: 
102: /**
103:  * Result of the hybrid capability discovery resolver.
104:  *
105:  * Discriminated by `source`:
106:  *   - "agent"   — agent reported capabilities via ACP `initialize` (authoritative).
107:  *   - "catalog" — agent silent; gatomia-maintained catalog supplied the values.
108:  *   - "none"    — neither source has values; selectors MUST be hidden.
109:  *
110:  * `thinkingLevels` and `agentRoles` are optional even on `"agent"` /
111:  * `"catalog"` results because not every provider exposes them — the
112:  * picker MUST hide the corresponding chip when the array is undefined
113:  * or empty.
114:  *
115:  * @see contracts/agent-capabilities-contract.md
116:  */
117: export type ResolvedCapabilities =
118: 	| {
119: 			source: "agent";
120: 			modes: ModeDescriptor[];
121: 			models: ModelDescriptor[];
122: 			thinkingLevels?: ThinkingLevelDescriptor[];
123: 			agentRoles?: AgentRoleDescriptor[];
124: 			acceptsFollowUp: boolean;
125: 	  }
126: 	| {
127: 			source: "catalog";
128: 			modes: ModeDescriptor[];
129: 			models: ModelDescriptor[];
130: 			thinkingLevels?: ThinkingLevelDescriptor[];
131: 			agentRoles?: AgentRoleDescriptor[];
132: 			acceptsFollowUp: boolean;
133: 	  }
134: 	| { source: "none" };
135: 
136: // ============================================================================
137: // Execution target
138: // ============================================================================
139: 
140: export type ExecutionTarget =
141: 	| { kind: "local" }
142: 	| { kind: "worktree"; worktreeId: string }
143: 	| { kind: "cloud"; providerId: string; cloudSessionId: string };
144:

146: // Worktree handle
147: // ============================================================================
148: 
149: export type WorktreeStatus = "created" | "in-use" | "abandoned" | "cleaned";
150: 
151: export interface WorktreeHandle {
152: 	/**
153: 	 * UUIDv4. Not necessarily equal to `sessionId`; v1 is 1:1 but the handle
154: 	 * exists as a separate entity so future specs can reuse worktrees across
155: 	 * sessions without a schema migration.

1: /**
2:  * RunningAgentsTreeProvider unit tests (T042, spec 018).
3:  *
4:  * TDD (Constitution III): red before T044.
5:  *
6:  * Contract coverage:
7:  *   - Root level emits three groups ("Active", "Recent", "Orphaned worktrees").
8:  *   - Active group lists sessions with non-terminal lifecycle state.
9:  *   - Recent group lists sessions with terminal lifecycle state.
10:  *   - Leaves carry `agent · mode · target · status` metadata + a click command
11:  *     targeting `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession`.
12:  *   - Worktree-backed sessions carry a `*-worktree` contextValue so the
13:  *     view/item/context menu can expose "Clean up worktree".
14:  *   - The provider refreshes when `AgentChatSessionStore.onDidChangeManifest`
15:  *     or `AgentChatRegistry.onDidChange` fires.
16:  */

20: import type { AgentChatRegistry } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry";
21: import type { AgentChatSessionStore } from "../../../src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store";

64: interface FakeStore {
65: 	manifestEmitter: EventEmitter<SessionManifest>;
66: 	active: AgentChatSession[];
67: 	recent: AgentChatSession[];
68: 	orphans: OrphanedWorktreeEntry[];
69: 	listActive(): Promise<AgentChatSession[]>;
70: 	listRecent(): Promise<AgentChatSession[]>;
71: 	listOrphanedWorktrees(): Promise<OrphanedWorktreeEntry[]>;
72: }

74: function createFakeStore(): FakeStore {
75: 	const manifestEmitter = new EventEmitter<SessionManifest>();
76: 	const fake: FakeStore = {
77: 		manifestEmitter,
78: 		active: [],
79: 		recent: [],
80: 		orphans: [],
81: 		listActive: () => Promise.resolve(fake.active),

83: 		listOrphanedWorktrees: () => Promise.resolve(fake.orphans),
84: 	};

102: 	item: RunningAgentsTreeItem,

105: ): { labelOk: boolean; stateOk: boolean; contextOk: boolean } {

109: 		contextOk: item.contextValue === expectedContextValue,

111: }

114: // Suite

116: 
117: describe("RunningAgentsTreeProvider (T042)", () => {
118: 	let store: FakeStore;

157: 				"Recent",
158: 				"Orphaned worktrees",
159: 			]);

161: 			const active = assertGroupShape(
162: 				children[1],
163: 				"Active",
164: 				"agent-chat-group-active"
165: 			);
166: 			const recent = assertGroupShape(
167: 				children[2],
168: 				"Recent",
169: 				"agent-chat-group-recent"
170: 			);
171: 			const orphans = assertGroupShape(
172: 				children[3],
173: 				"Orphaned worktrees",
174: 				"agent-chat-group-orphans"
175: 			);
176: 			expect(active).toEqual({
177: 				labelOk: true,
178: 				stateOk: true,
179: 				contextOk: true,
180: 			});
181: 			expect(recent).toEqual({
182: 				labelOk: true,
183: 				stateOk: true,

 * Contract coverage:
 *   - Root level emits three groups ("Active", "Recent", "Orphaned worktrees").

22: - `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts` already groups local agent-chat sessions into `Active`, `Recent`, and `Orphaned worktrees`, listens to both store and registry change events, and routes session clicks into `gatomia.agentChat.openForSession`.
23: - `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts` is already the canonical in-extension detail surface for local and restored agent-chat sessions, pushes session lists to the webview, and reuses the shared page registry plus `getWebviewContent()` bootstrap path.
24: - `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts` already projects stored cloud sessions into a provider-aware tree, including status icons, task children, PR links, and provider context keys.
25: - `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts` already handles one session-bound editor panel for agent chat, but its transport and hydration patterns are narrower than the sidebar view provider and should not become a second orchestration root.
26: - `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts` already serializes cloud session progress into a webview payload and supports refresh plus external-link actions.
27: - `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx` already defines the empty, loading, error, and populated rendering states the orchestration prototype can mirror instead of inventing a separate state vocabulary.
28: 
29: ## Smallest Viable Prototype
30: 
31: The smallest viable prototype is one normalized read model and one orchestration webview that sit on top of existing stores, registries, and page wiring.
32: 
33: - Reuse `AgentChatSessionStore` plus `AgentChatRegistry` as the local-session authority.
34: - Reuse `AgentSessionStorage` plus `ProviderRegistry` as the cloud-session authority.
35: - Reuse existing change emitters from those services to refresh both the tree surfaces and the orchestration surface from the same underlying state changes.
36: - Reuse the shared page registry and `getWebviewContent()` instead of introducing a second routing system or standalone webview bootstrap path.
37: - Reuse existing open-session commands so orchestration cards navigate back to `agent-chat` or the cloud-provider surface rather than replacing them.
38: 
39: ## Reused Modules
40: 
41: - `src/providers/running-agents-tree-provider.ts`
42: - `src/providers/agent-chat-view-provider.ts`
43: - `src/providers/cloud-agent-progress-provider.ts`
44: - `src/panels/agent-chat-panel.ts`
45: - `src/panels/cloud-agent-progress-panel.ts`
46: - `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-session-store.ts`
47: - `src/features/agent-chat/agent-chat-registry.ts`
48: - `src/features/cloud-agents/agent-session-storage.ts`
49: - `src/features/cloud-agents/provider-registry.ts`
50: - `ui/src/components/cloud-agents/cloud-agent-progress-view.tsx`
51: - `ui/src/page-registry.tsx`
52: - `src/utils/get-webview-content.ts`
53: 
54: ## New Modules
55: 
56: - `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` for the merged projection only.
57: - `src/providers/orchestration-view-provider.ts` for the orchestration webview binding and command bridge.
58: - `ui/src/features/orchestration/index.tsx` for the orchestration dashboard surface.
59: 
60: ## Target Slice
61: 
62: - Merge local agent-chat sessions and cloud-agent sessions into one projection with shared lifecycle buckets.
63: - Render four visible groups: `Active`, `Waiting`, `Completed`, and `Failed`.
64: - Surface current metadata that already exists or is trivially derivable now: session id, source, title, agent name, state, timestamps, worktree status, last visible activity, and blocking status.
65: - Keep the existing Running Agents and Cloud Agents trees intact while the orchestration view becomes an aggregated read-only overview.
66: - Route actions back into existing session-opening and refresh flows instead of creating orchestration-specific mutations.
67: 
68: ## Constraints
69: 
70: - No second orchestration state store.
71: - No new routing layer.
72: - No duplicate session lifecycle model.
73: - No orchestration-owned mutation path beyond delegating to existing commands.
74: - Cloud sessions should continue to open in their current best surface, including external URLs where that is the only richer detail view today.

4:  * Provider-agnostic session CRUD and read-only marking.

7:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/storage-interface.md

12: import type { Memento } from "./provider-config-store";

16: 	type AgentSession,

23: // ============================================================================
24: // Storage Key
25: // ============================================================================
26: 
27: const SESSIONS_KEY = "gatomia.cloudAgent.sessions";
28: 
29: // ============================================================================
30: // AgentSessionStorage
31: // ============================================================================
32: 
33: /**
34:  * Provider-agnostic session storage using VS Code workspace state.
35:  *
36:  * @see specs/016-multi-provider-agents/contracts/storage-interface.md
37:  */
38: export class AgentSessionStorage {
39: 	private readonly workspaceState: Memento;
40: 
41: 	constructor(workspaceState: Memento) {
42: 		this.workspaceState = workspaceState;
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	/**
46: 	 * Get all sessions for the current workspace.
47: 	 */
48: 	getAll(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
49: 		const raw = this.workspaceState.get<unknown[]>(SESSIONS_KEY);
50: 		if (!Array.isArray(raw)) {
51: 			return Promise.resolve([]);
52: 		}
53: 		const valid: AgentSession[] = [];
54: 		for (const entry of raw) {
55: 			if (isValidAgentSession(entry)) {
56: 				valid.push(normalizeSession(entry as AgentSession));
57: 			} else {
58: 				logDebug(
59: 					`Skipping invalid session entry: ${JSON.stringify(entry).slice(0, 200)}`
60: 				);
61: 			}
62: 		}
63: 		return Promise.resolve(valid);
64: 	}
65: 
66: 	/**
67: 	 * Get a single session by local ID.
68: 	 */
69: 	async getById(localId: string): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
70: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
71: 		return sessions.find((s) => s.localId === localId);
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	/**
75: 	 * Get sessions filtered by provider ID.
76: 	 */
77: 	async getByProvider(providerId: string): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
78: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
79: 		return sessions.filter((s) => s.providerId === providerId);
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	/**
83: 	 * Get active (non-terminal, non-read-only) sessions.
84: 	 */
85: 	async getActive(): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
86: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
87: 		return sessions.filter((s) => {
88: 			if (s.isReadOnly) {
89: 				return false;
90: 			}
91: 			switch (s.status) {
92: 				case SessionStatus.COMPLETED:
93: 				case SessionStatus.FAILED:
94: 				case SessionStatus.CANCELLED:

96: 				default:

102: 	/**
103: 	 * Check if a task (by specTaskId) is already in a non-terminal session.
104: 	 * Returns the running session if found, undefined otherwise.
105: 	 */
106: 	async findActiveBySpecTaskId(
107: 		specTaskId: string
108: 	): Promise<AgentSession | undefined> {
109: 		const active = await this.getActive();
110: 		return active.find((s) => s.tasks.some((t) => t.specTaskId === specTaskId));
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	/**
114: 	 * Save a new session.
115: 	 * @throws StoreError if localId already exists
116: 	 */
117: 	async create(session: AgentSession): Promise<void> {
118: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();

120: 			throw new StoreError(

122: 				StoreErrorCode.ALREADY_EXISTS,
123: 				"create"

125: 		}
126: 		sessions.push(session);
127: 		await this.persist(sessions);
128: 		logInfo(`Session created: ${session.localId} (${session.providerId})`);
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	/**
132: 	 * Update an existing session.
133: 	 * @throws StoreError if session not found
134: 	 */
135: 	async update(localId: string, updates: Partial<AgentSession>): Promise<void> {
136: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
137: 		const index = sessions.findIndex((s) => s.localId === localId);
138: 		if (index === -1) {
139: 			throw new StoreError(
140: 				`Session "${localId}" not found`,
141: 				StoreErrorCode.NOT_FOUND,
142: 				"update"
143: 			);
144: 		}
145: 		const cleaned = stripUndefined(updates);
146: 		sessions[index] = { ...sessions[index], ...cleaned, updatedAt: Date.now() };
147: 		await this.persist(sessions);
148: 		logDebug(`Session updated: ${localId}`);
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	/**
152: 	 * Delete a session.
153: 	 */
154: 	async delete(localId: string): Promise<void> {
155: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
156: 		const filtered = sessions.filter((s) => s.localId !== localId);
157: 		await this.persist(filtered);
158: 		logInfo(`Session deleted: ${localId}`);
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	/**
162: 	 * Mark all sessions from a provider as read-only.
163: 	 */
164: 	async markProviderReadOnly(providerId: string): Promise<void> {
165: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
166: 		for (const session of sessions) {
167: 			if (session.providerId === providerId) {
168: 				session.isReadOnly = true;
169: 			}
170: 		}
171: 		await this.persist(sessions);
172: 		logInfo(`All sessions for provider "${providerId}" marked read-only`);
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	/**
176: 	 * Get terminal sessions that need cleanup (updated before cutoff time).
177: 	 * Only returns sessions in completed/failed/cancelled state.
178: 	 */
179: 	async getForCleanup(cutoffTime: number): Promise<AgentSession[]> {
180: 		const sessions = await this.getAll();
181: 		return sessions.filter((s) => {
182: 			const isTerminal =
183: 				s.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ||
184: 				s.status === SessionStatus.FAILED ||
185: 				s.status === SessionStatus.CANCELLED;
186: 			if (!isTerminal) {
187: 				return false;
188: 			}
189: 			const completionTime = s.completedAt ?? s.updatedAt;
190: 			return completionTime < cutoffTime;
191: 		});
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	private async persist(sessions: AgentSession[]): Promise<void> {
195: 		await this.workspaceState.update(SESSIONS_KEY, sessions);
196: 	}
197: }
198: 
199: // ============================================================================
200: // Validation
201: // ============================================================================
202: 
203: /**
204:  * Runtime check that a deserialized value has the minimum required shape of an AgentSession.
205:  * Filters out corrupted or schema-incompatible entries after deserialization.
206:  */
207: /**
208:  * Remove undefined values from an object to prevent overwriting
209:  * existing fields (especially arrays like tasks/pullRequests) with undefined
210:  * during a partial update spread.
211:  */
212: function stripUndefined<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(obj: T): Partial<T> {
213: 	const result: Record<string, unknown> = {};
214: 	for (const key of Object.keys(obj)) {
215: 		if (obj[key] !== undefined) {
216: 			result[key] = obj[key];
217: 		}
218: 	}
219: 	return result as Partial<T>;
220: }
221: 
222: const TERMINAL_SESSION_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
223: 	SessionStatus.COMPLETED,
224: 	SessionStatus.FAILED,
225: 	SessionStatus.CANCELLED,
226: ]);
227: 
228: const TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
229: 	TaskStatus.COMPLETED,
230: 	TaskStatus.FAILED,
231: 	TaskStatus.SKIPPED,
232: ]);
233: 
234: const SESSION_TO_TASK_STATUS: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
235: 	[SessionStatus.COMPLETED]: TaskStatus.COMPLETED,
236: 	[SessionStatus.FAILED]: TaskStatus.FAILED,
237: 	[SessionStatus.CANCELLED]: TaskStatus.SKIPPED,
238: };
239: 
240: function normalizeSession(session: AgentSession): AgentSession {
241: 	if (!TERMINAL_SESSION_STATUSES.has(session.status)) {
242: 		return session;
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	let tasks: AgentTask[] | undefined;
246: 	const hasStaleTask = session.tasks.some(
247: 		(t) => !TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES.has(t.status)
248: 	);
249: 	if (hasStaleTask) {
250: 		const targetStatus =
251: 			SESSION_TO_TASK_STATUS[session.status] ?? TaskStatus.SKIPPED;
252: 		const now = Date.now();
253: 		tasks = session.tasks.map((task) => {
254: 			if (TERMINAL_TASK_STATUSES.has(task.status)) {
255: 				return task;
256: 			}
257: 			return { ...task, status: targetStatus, completedAt: now };
258: 		});
259: 	}
260: 
261: 	let pullRequests: PullRequest[] | undefined;
262: 	const hasUnknownPrState = session.pullRequests.some((pr) => !pr.state);
263: 	if (hasUnknownPrState) {
264: 		const defaultState =
265: 			session.status === SessionStatus.COMPLETED ? "merged" : "open";
266: 		pullRequests = session.pullRequests.map((pr) =>
267: 			pr.state ? pr : { ...pr, state: defaultState }
268: 		);
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	if (tasks || pullRequests) {
272: 		return {
273: 			...session,
274: 			...(tasks ? { tasks } : {}),
275: 			...(pullRequests ? { pullRequests } : {}),
276: 		};
277: 	}
278: 	return session;
279: }
280: 
281: function isValidAgentSession(value: unknown): boolean {
282: 	if (typeof value !== "object" || value === null) {
283: 		return false;
284: 	}
285: 	const obj = value as Record<string, unknown>;
286: 	return (
287: 		typeof obj.localId === "string" &&
288: 		typeof obj.providerId === "string" &&
289: 		typeof obj.status === "string" &&
290: 		typeof obj.branch === "string" &&
291: 		typeof obj.specPath === "string" &&
292: 		typeof obj.createdAt === "number" &&
293: 		typeof obj.updatedAt === "number" &&
294: 		Array.isArray(obj.tasks) &&
295: 		Array.isArray(obj.pullRequests)
296: 	);
297: }

I'm considering whether I need to expose the same normalized data to a tree view while keeping its behavior. The task specifies we should maintain the current tree-view experience but also connect it to a webview-friendly bridge for consistency. I wonder if I should refactor the running-agents-tree-provider to utilize a read model, but that might disrupt existing functionality. Alternatively, I could make it optional to use helper functions from the read model while ensuring both systems align.

**Exploring integration options**

I'm thinking the tree view can maintain its behavior while possibly utilizing a read model for active and recent agents. The current tree provider returns groups and leaves, so maybe I could instantiate OrchestrationReadModel optionally. However, the focus is on the read model, not the UI. I could integrate helper functions to allow both the running-agents-tree-provider and the read model to share lifecycles. I also need to check if the read model is used in the UI and identify any missing fields crucial for completion.

**Reviewing orchestration model**

I need to check the running-agents-prototype.md note about having a normalized read model along with a webview for orchestration over existing stores and registries. I'll look into the unit tests, feature tests, and current orchestration to see what’s minimal and where we can expand. Also, I should inspect `src/features/orchestration/orchestration-read-model.ts` for missing comments or types and consider adding a top-level comment. I’m thinking of importing ChatMessage and transcriptKeyFor, plus maybe user message types in the read model.

**Evaluating provider display names**

I'm considering whether to use `${session.providerId} · ${session.branch}`, but I'm unsure about that. Instead, it might make sense to include `agentName` for cloud services as the provider's display name if there's an active provider registry available. Right now, it seems I'm using providerId. Hmm, I wonder if I could derive that from the registry list? But is the provider registry API limited to getActive only? I should check out the provider-registry to see.

I’m patching the read model to fill the metadata gaps the checkbox calls out: better titles, better last-activity timestamps, and cleaner projections for cloud vs local sessions. Then I’ll extend the orchestration unit tests around those behaviors before running the quality gates.

27: 	private readonly configStore: ProviderConfigStore;

30: 	constructor(configStore: ProviderConfigStore) {
31: 		this.configStore = configStore;
32: 	}
33: 
34: 	/**
35: 	 * Register a provider adapter. Throws if a provider with the same ID is already registered.
36: 	 * @param provider - The provider to register
37: 	 */
38: 	register(provider: CloudAgentProvider): void {
39: 		const id = provider.metadata.id;
40: 		if (this.providers.has(id)) {
41: 			throw new Error(`Provider "${id}" is already registered`);
42: 		}
43: 		this.providers.set(id, provider);
44: 		logInfo(`Provider registered: ${id}`);
45: 	}
46:

48: 	 * Get a registered provider by ID.
49: 	 * @param id - The provider ID
50: 	 * @returns The provider or undefined if not found
51: 	 */
52: 	get(id: string): CloudAgentProvider | undefined {
53: 		return this.providers.get(id);
54: 	}
55: 
56: 	/**
57: 	 * Get all registered providers.
58: 	 * @returns Array of all registered providers
59: 	 */
60: 	getAll(): CloudAgentProvider[] {
61: 		return [...this.providers.values()];
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	/**
65: 	 * Get the currently active provider.
66: 	 * @returns The active provider or undefined if none is set
67: 	 */
68: 	getActive(): CloudAgentProvider | undefined {
69: 		if (!this.activeProviderId) {
70: 			return;
71: 		}
72: 		return this.providers.get(this.activeProviderId);
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	/**
76: 	 * Set the active provider. Throws if the provider is not registered.
77: 	 * @param id - The provider ID to activate
78: 	 */
79: 	async setActive(id: string): Promise<void> {
80: 		if (!this.providers.has(id)) {
81: 			throw new Error(`Provider "${id}" is not registered`);
82: 		}
83: 		this.activeProviderId = id;
84: 		await this.configStore.setActiveProvider(id);
85: 		logInfo(`Active provider set to: ${id}`);
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	/**
89: 	 * Clear the active provider.
90: 	 */
91: 	async clearActive(): Promise<void> {
92: 		this.activeProviderId = undefined;
93: 		await this.configStore.clearActiveProvider();
94: 		logInfo("Active provider cleared");
95: 	}

97: 	/**
98: 	 * Restore the active provider from the config store.
99: 	 * Called on extension activation to resume state.
100: 	 */

102: 		const storedId = await this.configStore.getActiveProvider();

9:  *   - Partition sessions into active vs. recent buckets for the tree view.
10:  *   - Drive the shutdown flush handed to `AgentChatSessionStore`.
11:  *
12:  * Persistence is out of scope for this module — the store owns disk state.
13:  * The registry is the authority on "what is live in this process right now".
14:  *
15:  * @see specs/018-agent-chat-panel/contracts/agent-chat-panel-protocol.md §2
